about.title: About
about.subtitle: The Story Behind the Music
about.bio_paragraph_1: Alexandros Tsopozidis was born on January 1, 1986, in the village of Sameba (Guniakala), Georgia. Of Pontic Greek heritage, he grew up surrounded by the rich musical traditions of the Caucasus region, where Eastern melodies intertwined with Greek folk rhythms.
about.bio_paragraph_2: In 1998, at the age of 12, Alexandros moved with his family to Paphos, Cyprus, where he began a new chapter of his life. Initially drawn to athletics, he trained at a Cyprus football academy with dreams of a professional sports career.
about.bio_paragraph_3: At his parents' request, Alexandros left football behind and discovered his true calling — music. In 2011, he began his musical journey in earnest, and his talent quickly caught the attention of Russian producer Kemran Amirov, who helped launch his career.
about.bio_paragraph_4: His debut duet with Eldar Dalgatov, 'Может ты вернёшься,' introduced him to Russian-speaking audiences. But it was his collaboration with Elbrus Dzhanmirzoev on 'Бродяга' that catapulted him to fame, amassing over 22 million views on YouTube.
about.bio_paragraph_5: Today, Alexandros performs in Russian, Greek, and Pontic Greek, blending pop with Caucasian and Eastern influences into a unique sound. Based in Paphos, Cyprus, he continues to release new music, with his latest single 'Mia Kardia' (2025) and 'Вечная любовь' coming soon. He is the recipient of the 9 Волна award for his contribution to ethnic music.
